text.content:
Player Cheat Sheet

This journal is the system’s searchable rules pocketbook for Ars Magica 2nd Edition (AG0201). Open it from the Rules Reference compendium, or link it on a character via Advanced → Links / the sheet Rules button.

What to roll

  • Simple die — routine tasks with no risk of spectacular failure.
  • Stress die — contested, dangerous, magical, or dramatic actions (combat, casting, most adventure rolls).

Core formulas (sheet does the math)

  • Ability check = Die + Characteristic + Ability (+1 if specialty applies).
  • Formulaic casting = Die + Technique + Form + Stamina vs spell Level.
  • Spontaneous casting = Die + Technique + Form + Intelligence, then ÷2 (fatiguing) or ÷5 (careful).
  • Attack = AtkB + Dex + Weapon Ability. Defense = ParB + Ability − Size. Damage = WpnDam + Str + Ability. Soak = Sta + armor Protection.

Confidence

Spend 1 Confidence for +3 on a roll when the sheet or casting dialog offers it.

Compendiums you’ll use

  • Abilities, Virtues & Flaws, Formulaic Spells, Weapons & Armor — drag onto characters.
  • Ability Reference / Order of Hermes — lore and ability notes.
  • Bestiary — sample creatures (adapt ArM5 framing to 2e combat as needed).

The die

Ars Magica uses a ten-sided die numbered 0–9. Foundry’s physical d10 shows a 10 face; this system maps that face to ArM 0 for math and chat.

Simple die

  • Roll once.
  • Face 0 counts as 10.
  • No botch, no explode.

Stress die

  • On the first roll, face 0 is a potential botch (die value 0). The storyguide chooses how many botch dice to roll and resolves the outcome.
  • Face 1 explodes: multiply the next face by 2, then 4, and so on for each further 1.
  • After an explode, a later face 0 counts as 10 (then multiply).
  • Otherwise the face value (2–9, or 10 after explode) × current multiplier is the die result.

Reading a chat roll

Chat cards show each die step, the base modifier, wound/fatigue condition lines (on stress rolls), Confidence if spent, and the final total. Potential botches and exploding stress dice are flagged on the card.

Specialties

When an ability’s specialty clearly applies, add +1 to that roll (check the specialty box on ability rolls when offered).

Confidence

Default saga rule in this system: spend 1 Confidence → +3 on the roll. You must have a Confidence point remaining. Magi and companions typically start with Confidence; grogs usually do not.

When you cannot act

If wounds or fatigue reach Incapacitated / Unconscious, stress rolls are blocked and chat explains why.

Characteristics

Eight characteristics. Positive is talented; negative is a hindrance. Typical starting range is about −3 to +3.

  • Intelligence (Int) — reasoning, memory; spontaneous magic.
  • Perception (Per) — noticing, searching, awareness.
  • Strength (Str) — force, carrying; weapon damage; encumbrance threshold.
  • Stamina (Sta) — endurance; formulaic casting; Soak.
  • Presence (Pre) — impression, leadership, presence-based social rolls.
  • Communication (Com) — speech, writing, teaching, persuasion.
  • Dexterity (Dex) — fine motor skill; weapon Attack.
  • Quickness (Qik) — speed; First Strike; Dodge.

Abilities

Abilities are Items on the character (Talents, Skills, Knowledges). Score is the number added to rolls. Import from the Abilities pack or the Character Wizard.

  • Roll = Die + Characteristic + Ability (+1 specialty when applicable).
  • Weapon abilities (Brawl, Single Weapon, Great Weapon, Bow, Thrown Weapon, Crossbow) feed combat totals when linked on the weapon.
  • Dodge feeds the Dodge defense total.

Advancement (reminder)

Experience and seasonal study follow the saga’s AG0201 advancement rules. Track XP on ability Items; the sheet stores scores—the storyguide adjudicates study totals and Art advances.

Derived combat totals

Equipped weapons and armor drive the Combat tab. Formulas match AG0201 Ch. 3 as implemented by the sheet:

  • First Strike = Weapon Speed + Quickness + Attack Skill − Encumbrance
  • Attack = Weapon Atk Bonus + Dexterity + Attack Skill
  • Damage = Weapon Damage + Strength + Attack Skill
  • Defense (parry) = Weapon Parry Bonus + Parry Skill − Size
  • Dodge = Dodge Ability + Quickness − Encumbrance − Size
  • Soak = Stamina + sum of equipped armor Protection

Attack Skill / Parry Skill come from the weapon’s linked Ability Item when set; otherwise from the weapon’s stored skill fields.

Encumbrance

Encumbrance = max(0, Total Load − Strength). Load sums equipped weapons, armor, and gear (plus any Extra Load on the sheet). Encumbrance subtracts from First Strike and Dodge.

Resolving a blow (table flow)

  1. Compare First Strike / initiative order as the storyguide prefers.
  2. Attacker rolls stress die + Attack; defender uses Defense or Dodge as appropriate.
  3. If the attack hits, compare Damage to Soak; excess determines wound severity per the saga’s wound chart.
  4. Apply the new wound level on the status strip; penalties flow into later stress rolls automatically.

Weapons & Armor pack

Names bake in Sp / Atk / Dam / Par (or Protection / Load for armor) for quick browsing. AG0201 chart numbers win where editions overlap. Extra Lords of Men gear is prefixed LoM — (ArM5-scale reference).

Wound levels

Tracked on the character status strip (every sheet tab). Body penalties apply to most stress rolls and appear in the chat breakdown.

LevelPenalty
Unhurt0
Hurt0
Light Wounds−1
Medium Wounds−3
Heavy Wounds−5
IncapacitatedCannot act
UnconsciousCannot act

Fatigue levels

LevelPenalty
Fresh0
Winded0
Weary−1
Tired−3
Dazed−5
UnconsciousCannot act

Casting and fatigue

  • Formulaic: if casting total < Level, lose 1 Fatigue (spell may still work unless more than 10 short).
  • Spontaneous fatiguing: always lose 1 Fatigue when you cast that way.
  • Spontaneous careful: no Fatigue from the casting mode itself.

Recovery, wound treatment, and long-term fatigue are storyguide calls from the core book.

Formulaic spells

Cast from a spell Item on the character (import from Formulaic Spells).

Casting modifier = Technique + Form + Stamina.

Roll die + modifier and compare to the spell’s Level:

  • Total ≥ Level — spell succeeds; no Fatigue from failure.
  • Total < Level — lose 1 Fatigue; the spell still takes effect unless the total is more than 10 short (then it fails entirely).

Spontaneous spells

Click a Technique/Form cell on the Arts grid.

Casting modifier = Technique + Form + Intelligence.

  • Fatiguing — Level equivalent = (die + modifier) ÷ 2; always costs 1 Fatigue.
  • Careful — Level equivalent = (die + modifier) ÷ 5; no Fatigue.

The divided result is the highest Level-equivalent effect you can manage. Penetration and raw power use the undivided total as the storyguide requires.

Voice, gestures, auras

Situational modifiers (loud voice, no gestures, hostile aura, etc.) are applied as storyguide modifiers on top of the sheet totals for now.

Rituals & vis

Ritual casting times, vis costs, and raw vis improvisation follow the Magic chapter. Track pawns and long-term Fatigue narratively until dedicated UI lands.

Magic Resistance

Compare Penetration to the target’s resistance (Form + other factors per saga rules). Creature Might and Parma Magica details are in AG0201; adapt bestiary Might scores when using ArM5 samples.

Items

  • Abilities — talents, skills, and knowledges for the sheet and wizard.
  • Virtues & Flaws — AG0201 catalog with signed point costs (+1…+5 / −1…−5), filed in Foundry folders by cost.
  • Formulaic Spells — AG0201 Chapter 5 spells (2e) plus ArM5 Chapter IX / index fillers where 2e had no matching title. Arts, level, and text for drag-and-drop casting; filed under Technique → Form.
  • Grand Grimoire (Arm5 Ref) — optional locked Arm5 community compilation (~1,100+ spells with full R/D/T and sources). Reference only; keep using Formulaic Spells for 2e play. Do not mass-import into the world.
  • Weapons & Armor — AG0201 combat charts plus non-conflicting LoM extras (LoM — prefix).

Actors

  • Sample Covenants — starter covenant Actors.
  • Bestiary (ArM5 Ch.13) — sample Magic, Faerie, Infernal, and Divine creatures; convert Init/Atk/Def/Dam and Might framing to 2e at the table.

Journals

  • Rules Reference — this journal.
  • Ability Reference — ability category notes.
  • Order of Hermes — Order / Houses / Tribunals overview.
  • Covenant Template — blank chronicle pages for your saga.

Edition policy

Combat and casting math are 2e / AG0201. Later-edition OGL material appears only as reference data when it does not override 2e numbers.

Based on Ars Magica material ©1993–2024, licensed by Trident, Inc. d/b/a Atlas Games®, under CC-BY-SA 4.0 where OGL sources apply. Ars Magica and related marks are trademarks of Trident, Inc.

Licensed PDF (not shipped)

The Ars Magica 2e core book PDF is not included in this system. If you own AG0201 (or another licensed PDF), attach it for full chapter text:

  1. Place the PDF in your Foundry user data or world assets folder.
  2. Open the Journal directory → right-click → Attach Rules PDF….
  3. Choose the file. Foundry creates ArM2 Core Rules (PDF) with a PDF journal page.

Characters can drop that journal onto Rules Journal UUID (Advanced — Links), or use the header Rules button once linked. World settings also store the PDF path and journal UUID.

Keep this HTML Rules Reference as the quick table companion; use the PDF when you need full chapter wording, examples, and charts.
